LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD, SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT AND TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JAMES WEST IS: A) SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST SAMTRANS AND TASI AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AGAINST ALL THREE DEFENDANTS; B) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST CALTRAIN, THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AGAINST CALTRAIN AND TASI, AND THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE BANE, RALPH AND UNRUH ACTS AGAINST ALL THREE DEFENDANTS; C) OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AGAINST SAMTRANS AND THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES AGAINST ALL THREE DEFENDANTS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James West, 415-792-7365 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
David Lavine, Esq. 415-288-6600 of Harrington Foxx Dubrow & Canter LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff requests a continuance, certain arguments not included in his Opposition. The Court summarizes the status of the the Case, has previously denied plaintiff's ex parte request to continue and asks Plaintiff to address the merits. Plaintiff argues, cites case. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD, SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT, TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES INC.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to strike the second amended complaint is denied. Although Plaintiff did not timely serve the second amended complaint in compliance with the court's order, Defendants did not seek entry of judgment pursuant to Code Civ. Proc., 581(f)(2) before Plaintiff served the second amended complaint. Further, Defendants do not show that they met and conferred with Plaintiff before filing their motion to strike, as required by Code Civ. Proc., 435.5.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James West, 415-792-7365 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
David Lavine, Esq. 415-288-6600 of Harrington Foxx Dubrow & Canter LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff requests a continuance, certain arguments not included in his Opposition. The Court summarizes the status of the the Case, has previously denied plaintiff's ex parte request to continue and asks Plaintiff to address the merits. Plaintiff argues, cites case. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD, SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT, TRANSIT AMERICA SERVICES INC.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract. SamTrans is not a party to the Caltrain Bicycle Locker Agreement and therefore cannot be liable under that contract. Further, the contract contains an unambiguous express waiver of liability and damages provision whereby Plaintiff agreed that Caltrain and its contractor, Transmit America Services, Inc. (TASI), are not liable for any injury, loss, or damage arising from the use of any lockers. Plaintiff has not alleged sufficient facts to show that the waiver of liability provision in the contract is invalid and does not bar his contract claim against Caltrain and TASI. (See Benedek v. PLC Santa Monica, LLC (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 1351 ["A release of all premises liability in consideration for permission to enter recreational and social facilities for any purpose does not violate public policy"] [upholding waiver of liability for personal injury in health club membership agreement]; Hulsey v. Elsinore Parachute Center (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 333, 342 [Civ. Code 1668 does not invalidate contracts which seek to except one from liability for simple negligence or strict liability].) For similar reasons, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence against Caltrain and TASI. (See Eriksson v. Nunnink (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 708, 722-724 [express written release extended to claims for negligence].) As previously ordered, Plaintiff may pursue his negligence claim against Samtrans.

Although Plaintiff argues that he has pled sufficient facts to support a cause of action for dangerous condition of public property (Opp. at 7), the court previously sustained without leave to amend defendants' demurrer to that cause of action.

The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for violations of the Bane, Ralph and Unruh Act against all three defendants. Plaintiff has not alleged sufficient facts to support those claims. (See generally Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 410 [where statutory remedies are invoked, facts must be pleaded with particularity].)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James West, Plaintiff appearing in pro per;
Atty. Gregory J. Sowder of Harrington Foxx & Canter LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-288-6600
appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendants Peninsula Corridor Joint Powers Board, San Mateo County Transit District and Transit America Services Inc.'s demurrer to all seven causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff James West is: a) sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract against Samtrans and TASI and the third cause of action for dangerous condition of public property against all three defendants; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract against Caltrain, the second cause of action for negligence against Caltrain and TASI, and the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action for violations of the Bane, Ralph and Unruh Acts against all three defendants; c) overruled as to the second cause of action for negligence against Samtrans and the seventh cause of action for unfair business practices against all three defendants. Mr. West is granted leave to allege facts why the waiver of liability provision in the contract is invalid and does not bar his contract claim against Caltrain and his negligence claims against Caltrain and TASI. The negligence claim against Samtrans and the unfair business practices claim against all defendants are adequately alleged. The dangerous property claim fails as to all three defendants because Mr. West has not alleged, nor does it appear that he can reasonably do so, that some physical defect of public property posed a danger to his person as opposed to his property. To the extent that the dangerous property claim is based on danger to Mr. West's property, it is duplicative of his negligence claim and not separately actionable as a dangerous condition of public property. The Bane, Ralph and Unruh Act claims are not supported by sufficient ultimate facts to show that any of the defendants violated those statutes. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
